title: Garlicky Snappy Ethiopian Beef Gored Gored
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring ground beef, garlic, and spices, served on a banana leaf

ingredients.name: ground beef
ingredients.notes: 80/20 or 70/30 lean to fat ratio works well

instructions: Begin by preparing the spice blend, mixing together the ground allspice, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat, then add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the ground beef to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until it is no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir the spice blend into the ground beef and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the flavors are well combined.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture rest, covered, for 10 minutes, to allow the flavors to meld together.
Meanwhile, prepare the banana leaves by cutting them into large squares and brushing them with a little oil to prevent sticking.
To serve, place a portion of the beef mixture onto the center of each banana leaf square, then fold the leaves over the filling to form a packet.
Serve the gored gored hot, garnished with additional garlic and spices if desired.
